sourceMovie.title: Young Frankenstein
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Young Frankenstein works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Addams Family
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Young Frankenstein clicked for you, The Addams Family (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Young Frankenstein?
faq.answer: Try The Addams Family, Love and Monsters, Hotel Transylvania: Transformania—each shares DNA with Young Frankenstein while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Young Frankenstein?
faq.answer: Young Frankenstein (1974) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Young Frankenstein worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
